response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished polyglot engineer, but his profile fundamentally mismatches this role: his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, versus the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang systems work, his database experience is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure (RIDE Capital was a German fintech using Node/GraphQL, not capital markets systems). Strong communication skills and CI/CD familiarity cannot offset the absence of both mandatory pillars.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing track record (conference talks, workshops, courses)
Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and cross-platform breadth
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, far below the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle background; database work is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
